name: Strep-tag / Twin-Strep-tag affinity system
description: The Strep-tag system is a compact peptide-affinity technology built around engineered streptavidin variants. The core tag, Strep-tag II (WSHPQFEK, 8 aa), was derived by rational engineering from the original Strep-tag selected by Schmidt and Skerra in 1993 and binds Strep-Tactin — a loop-engineered streptavidin — with a Kd of ~1 µM, enabling single-step purification under physiological conditions with mild desthiobiotin elution. The Twin-Strep-tag (SAWSHPQFEK-GGGSGGGSGGSAWSHPQFEK, 28 aa) links two Strep-tag II units via a flexible GGGSGGGSGGS spacer; avid bivalent binding to Strep-Tactin XT brings the apparent affinity into the picomolar range while retaining reversibility, making the system suitable for demanding applications such as mammalian cell-culture purification, surface plasmon resonance capture, and cell-sorting. Both tags are bioinert, tolerate a wide range of buffer conditions, and can be placed at either terminus of a recombinant protein.
